tasks.title: Establish extraction-ready service boundaries without rolling out standalone services yet
tasks.prompt: Treat PR #581 and its follow-up as a service-boundary program inside the monolith, not a full microservice rollout. The current goal is stable domain service interfaces, thin handlers, repository boundaries, auth facades, and OpenAPI-backed contracts so local implementations can later be swapped for remote ones with minimal churn. Do not add standalone service processes, Docker or Kubernetes rollout, HTTP client adapters, mode toggles, or strangler deployment in this wave.
notes: This captures the overall nuance from the later 6-step extraction plan: use the plan as design context, but stop this TASKS.json at boundary establishment.
Step 1 (freeze interfaces) and step 2 (keep the HTTP/OpenAPI contract stable) are in scope as architectural constraints. Steps 3-6 are future extraction work, not current acceptance criteria.

Cross-service concerns such as DB-per-service ownership, forwarded JWT verification, idempotency keys, retries, observability headers and metrics, and event buses should inform boundary design now but are not part of the current rollout.

tasks.title: Preserve extraction-ready handler -> domain -> repository boundaries
tasks.prompt: Preserve the PR #581 backend architecture boundary: handlers stay HTTP-only, domain services own business logic and orchestration, repositories own persistence, and container wiring owns dependency injection. Keep callers depending on stable service interfaces so the markets slice can be extracted later with minimal churn, but do not swap to remote implementations in this wave.
notes: Source: PR #581 body at https://github.com/openpredictionmarkets/socialpredict/pull/581.
Completed according to the PR body: domain layer, repository pattern, DTO pattern, service interfaces, error mapping, and build integrity.
This task maps to the 'freeze the interface' part of the later extraction plan: callers should depend on service interfaces now so a later local-versus-remote swap is low churn.
Out of scope for this task: standalone service binaries, generated remote clients, runtime mode toggles, and side-by-side deployment.

tasks.title: Complete the markets handler migration to thin service-backed endpoints
tasks.prompt: Keep markets HTTP handlers thin and service-backed. Preserve DTO mapping, AuthService-based authorization, and the rule that handlers do not reach directly into GORM, models, or util.GetDB.
notes: Completed according to the PR body: handlers/markets/handler.go, createmarket.go, listmarkets.go, getmarkets.go, marketdetailshandler.go, marketprojectedprobability.go, resolvemarket.go, and searchmarkets.go.
The PR body marks createmarket.go as the reference pattern: request DTO -> domain request -> service call -> error mapping -> response DTO.
Later review comments keep this migration as baseline and ask for follow-up cleanup in remaining high-complexity handlers rather than undoing the architecture.

tasks.title: Complete the users handler migration to service-backed flows
tasks.prompt: Keep user-facing handlers as thin HTTP facades over the users and markets domain services. Preserve auth via the auth facade and keep handler code out of direct database access.
notes: Completed according to the PR body: handlers/users/apply_transaction.go removal plus changedescription.go, changedisplayname.go, changeemoji.go, changepassword.go, changepersonallinks.go, financial.go, listusers.go, privateuser/privateuser.go, publicuser.go, publicuser/portfolio.go, publicuser/publicuser.go, userpositiononmarkethandler.go, and credit/usercredit.go.
The PR body says handler/service routing is complete and profile, portfolio, credit, and financial flows are already domain-backed.
Later thread guidance says users/errors.go and users/transactions.go should stay package-level unless behavior or API contracts justify deeper splitting.

tasks.title: Complete the bets, math, and auth-facade migration baseline
tasks.prompt: Preserve the completed migration baseline where bets handlers are thin service clients, reusable market math stays in domain packages, and authentication is routed through internal/service/auth rather than legacy middleware or direct DB reach-through from handlers.
notes: Completed according to the PR body: handlers/bets/betshandler.go, buying/buypositionhandler.go, selling/sellpositionhandler.go, removal of listbetshandler.go and legacy bets helpers, and migration of middleware/auth_legacy.go into internal/service/auth.

Completed according to the PR body: financials/workprofits.go, market/dust.go, market/marketvolume.go, outcomes/dbpm/marketshares.go, payout/resolvemarketcore.go, positions/positionsmath.go and related modules, positions/adjust_valuation.go, positions/earliest_users.go, and probabilities/wpam/*.go moved behind services or pure domain math helpers.
This task records the migration baseline only. Remaining bets, math, and dependency-cleanup follow-up work appears in later pending tasks.

tasks.title: Keep server routes and OpenAPI inventory aligned
tasks.prompt: Keep backend route behavior aligned with backend/docs/openapi.yaml. Preserve the contract-based API inventory established in PR #581, including the canonical resolve route and the legacy create alias.
notes: Source: OpenAPI section of the PR body.

The PR body marks the listed server.go routes and backend/docs/openapi.yaml routes as aligned, including GET /v0/global/leaderboard, GET /v0/markets/* variants, POST /v0/markets/{id}/resolve, POST /v0/bet, POST /v0/sell, and POST /v0/create as a legacy alias.
This task covers the current contract-discipline baseline. Keep the markets HTTP contract stable enough to back a later generated client or remote adapter without implementing that adapter yet.
If markets is extracted later, a service-scoped OpenAPI artifact can be introduced then; for this wave the contract of record remains backend/docs/openapi.yaml.
